result:
14 confirmed. That's a total of 35 + 14 = 49 "whole" functions, and 9 "partial" functions — 58 functions total.

## CONFIG_SAE — functions whose compiled behavior depends on it (src/ tree)

**Method**: grepped all 31 `CONFIG_SAE` occurrences in `src/**/*.{c,h}` (verified none are near-miss tokens like `CONFIG_SAE_PK`), mapped each `#ifdef`/`#endif` pair's line range against `ccodegraph`'s function `line_start`/`line_end` per file, and separately checked `src/common/Makefile` / `wpa_supplicant/Makefile` for file-level gating (a blind spot plain `#ifdef` grep misses).

### Key finding: `src/common/sae.c` is entirely file-gated, not `#ifdef`-gated
`sae.c` contains **zero** internal `#ifdef CONFIG_SAE` lines — but `wpa_supplicant/Makefile:241-244` only adds `../src/common/sae.o` to `OBJS` inside `ifdef CONFIG_SAE`. So every function in the file only exists in the binary when `CONFIG_SAE` is on. That makes all **35** of its functions "whole": `sae_set_group, sae_clear_temp_data, sae_clear_data, buf_shift_right, sae_get_rand, sae_get_rand_and_mask, sae_pwd_seed_key, get_rand_1_to_p_1, is_quadratic_residue_blind, sae_test_pwd_seed_ecc, sae_test_pwd_seed_ffc, get_random_qr_qnr, sae_derive_pwe_ecc, sae_derive_pwe_ffc, sae_derive_commit_element_ecc, sae_derive_commit_element_ffc, sae_derive_commit, sae_prepare_commit, sae_derive_k_ecc, sae_derive_k_ffc, sae_derive_keys, sae_process_commit, sae_write_commit, sae_group_allowed, sae_parse_commit_token, sae_parse_commit_scalar, sae_parse_commit_element_ecc, sae_parse_commit_element_ffc, sae_parse_commit_element, sae_parse_commit, sae_cn_confirm, sae_cn_confirm_ecc, sae_cn_confirm_ffc, sae_write_confirm, sae_check_confirm`.

### "whole" — `src/ap/ieee802_11.c:318-876` (single big in-file `#ifdef`, 14 functions)
`auth_build_sae_commit, auth_build_sae_confirm, auth_sae_send_commit, auth_sae_send_confirm, use_sae_anti_clogging, check_sae_token, auth_build_token_req, sae_check_big_sync, auth_sae_retransmit_timer, sae_clear_retransmit_timer, sae_set_retransmit_timer, sae_sm_step, handle_auth_sae, auth_sae_init_committed`

Note: `sae_clear_retransmit_timer` has a special twist — `src/ap/ieee802_11.h:97-105` declares the real function under `CONFIG_SAE` but supplies a `static inline` no-op stub under `#else`. So it's a real "whole" function with a compile-time no-op shadow, not a "partial" body split.

**Total whole: 35 + 14 = 49 functions.**

### "partial" — function always compiled, only part of the body is conditional (9 functions)

| Function | File | ifdef line(s) | What's gated |
|---|---|---|---|
| `wpa_write_rsn_ie` | src/ap/wpa_auth_ie.c | 191-202 | adds SAE/FT-SAE AKM suites to RSN IE |
| `wpa_validate_wpa_ie` | src/ap/wpa_auth_ie.c | 524-529, 606-611 (two separate regions) | SAE AKM selector parsing/matching |
| `hostapd_ctrl_iface_sta_mib` | src/ap/ctrl_iface_ap.c | 148-155 | emits `sae_group=` status line |
| `handle_auth` | src/ap/ieee802_11.c | 954-957, 1180-1200 (two separate regions) | accepts `WLAN_AUTH_SAE` alg / dispatches to `handle_auth_sae` |
| `check_assoc_ies` | src/ap/ieee802_11.c | 1538-1560 | validates PMKSA caching / AKM vs. auth_alg for SAE |
| `ap_free_sta` | src/ap/sta_info.c | 313-316 | frees `sta->sae` state |
| `hostapd_wpa_auth_get_psk` | src/ap/wpa_auth_glue.c | 231-237 | returns SAE-derived PMK instead of PSK |
| `rsn_key_mgmt_to_bitfield` | src/common/wpa_common.c | 485-490 | maps SAE/FT-SAE RSN selectors to bitfield |
| `wpa_gen_wpa_ie_rsn` | src/rsn_supp/wpa_ie.c | 173-178 | writes SAE/FT-SAE AKM selector into generated RSN IE |

**Total partial: 9 functions.**

### Grand total: **58 functions** (49 whole + 9 partial) across `src/ap`, `src/common`, `src/rsn_supp`.

### Non-function CONFIG_SAE sites (excluded from the count, noted for completeness)
- `src/ap/hostapd.h:268-273` — struct field members (`sae_token_key`, `dot11RSNASAERetransPeriod`, etc.) in `struct hostapd_data`, not a function.
- `src/ap/sta_info.h:162-164` — `struct sae_data *sae` field in `struct sta_info`, not a function.

These don't gate a function body themselves, but since they change struct layout only when `CONFIG_SAE` is on, any function touching those fields (e.g. the ones above) is implicitly SAE-coupled at the ABI level even outside its own `#ifdef` region — flagged here since it's a real blind spot, but not counted as a separate "function depends on CONFIG_SAE" entry since the task asks about `#ifdef CONFIG_SAE` blocks specifically.

No other `src/` files reference `CONFIG_SAE` (31 raw occurrences fully accounted for above); `mesh_rsn.c`, which also calls into SAE code, lives under `wpa_supplicant/`, outside the `src/` tree scope of this task.
